project_common.art_style: 8-bit pixel art, retro minimalist aesthetic inspired by Google Chrome offline game
project_common.rendering: pixel perfect rendering with no anti-aliasing

shared_attributes.style: 8-bit pixel art with sharp, dangerous silhouette
shared_attributes.shading: flat color, no gradients or shading
shared_attributes.movement: scrolls left at current game speed

context.role: Airborne obstacle requiring player to duck or time jump carefully
context.screen_position: Spawns at x=650px (screen_width + 50), y=50px or y=80px (two height variants), scrolls left
context.usage_scenario: Appears after score 200+, forces player to use duck action, flies at two possible heights (Low: 50px, High: 80px)

visual_specification.shape: Pteranodon-style flying reptile with long beak, extended wings in flight pose
visual_specification.texture: solid pixel art, no texture patterns

animation.description: Wing flapping animation - frame 1: wings up position, frame 2: wings down position, creates flying motion

visual_specification.details: Long pointed beak extending forward, wings spread wide in flight, small body, distinctive pteranodon crest on head, clear wing membrane shape

relationships.shared_with: Same color (#535353) as all other game objects
relationships.differences_from: Only flying obstacle, only obstacle with animation (2 frames @ 10fps), spawns at elevated y positions (50px or 80px) unlike ground-based cacti (0px)

explicit_negations: No open beak or facial expressions
No motion blur or speed lines
No background elements integrated into sprite
Not static - must have 2-frame wing flapping animation
Not ground-based - always spawns at elevated y position
